Headline: Founder | Geospatial & Climate Intelligence Advisory | Former CTO (M&A Exit)
Summary: I build geospatial intelligence systems that translate land, climate, and satellite data into strategic decision-making tools.With a background in agronomy and over a decade leading technology and R&D in remote sensing, GIS, and large-scale data infrastructure, I operate at the intersection of environmental science, spatial systems, and climate strategy.As Co-Founder and CTO of Agrosatélite Applied Geotechnology — later acquired by Serasa Experian — I led the design and delivery of satellite monitoring products, spatial analytics pipelines, and enterprise geospatial architectures serving agribusiness, financial institutions, NGOs, and public agencies.My work has contributed to nationally relevant initiatives such as MapBiomas and Brazil’s Fourth National Communication to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), particularly in land use, land-use change and forestry (LULUCF) emissions reporting.Today, through my advisory practice, I help organizations design and implement:• Geospatial and WebGIS architectures• Satellite-based land use monitoring systems• Climate and carbon intelligence frameworks• Scalable spatial data infrastructure• AI-driven environmental analyticsI am particularly interested in projects that require technical depth, strategic clarity, and long-term systems thinking in climate and territorial intelligence.
